purpose: Tie the active P9 governance batch to final convergence gates, commit checkpoints, ignored runtime paths, skip-report policy, known risks, and release-blocking verification.

aiflowExecution.goflyImpact: P9 tasks were submitted through aiflow and completed locally with per-task commits because aiflow project policy disables commit and push; the current agent or human owns commit and push.

previousBatchHandoff.reason: R8 convergence was carried forward into the P9 active batch convergence contract after post-R8 framework gap governance completed.
previousBatchHandoff.completionPolicy: complete GOFLY-GOV-10P9-10 through aiflow after make governance-boundary-inventory-check, make governance-report-check, and make governance-10-rounds pass or document any environment-limited downgrade, with commits owned by the current agent or human

knownRisks.status: static topology passes; live compose can be skipped when the local Docker credential helper is unavailable
knownRisks.followUp: Run the Docker-backed reference app in CI or a host with docker-credential-desktop available before treating live dependency proof as complete.

knownRisks.status: REST allocations and promoted OpenAPI/governance latency are blocking; RPC latency remains report-only, while gateway and cache now have P9 ownership evidence without premature latency claims.
knownRisks.followUp: Promote only after five baseline samples, three current trend samples, allocation budget evidence, explicit latency ratio, and rollback guidance exist.

knownRisks.status: .aiflow, .harness, .tmp-test, .trae, coverage.out, and docs/superpowers remain runtime or local evidence paths and must not be committed.
knownRisks.followUp: Keep runtime evidence ignored and stage only durable governance contracts or source changes.

nextRoundRecommendations.action: Promote selected RPC unary and stream surfaces only after one release train records resolver, balancer, stream, benchmark, and Kitex rollback evidence.
Expand generated project compatibility fixtures with old, current, and future profiles that repeatedly generate, compile, test, and classify diffs.
Run Docker-backed reference app, cloud-native render, and release evidence gates in CI environments that provide Docker, Helm, and security scanning tools.
